digest.greeting: Friday, June 26, Jean. Today is anchored by the 10:00 AM weekly deck update, the 1:00 PM EPMO EOW check-in, and the 3:00 PM Executive Project Status Slide Update, with Sara and David both showing OOO and Mar out for the afternoon. Outlook shows 7 practical calendar blocks, 3 carried-over items, 0 reminders due today, and 4 urgent items. Keep the day focused on closing the KPI/LeadUp cleanup, keeping the July 1 transition follow-ups clean, making sure the weekly executive updates are publication-ready, and checking Sprint #13 capacity signals before the weekend. Broad Teams recent-thread discovery returned a Microsoft 403, so this brief uses the accessible chat list plus targeted thread fetches for EOW, Sprint #13, EPMO Liderazgo, and COI/Send Letters Queue context.

items.label: Turn the KPI set into SMART goals
items.detail: Still the highest-value open item. Sara shared the PE KPI structure and Jorge confirmed the midyear path is to set KPIs in Rippling and hold 1:1s so each team member understands the year-end rating basis. Use today’s protected time to turn the KPI set into concrete SMART goals before Monday’s midyear review block.

items.label: Schedule July 1 alignment follow-ups
items.detail: The new EPMO reporting structure is now formally announced, effective July 1. Keep the follow-ups with Thiago, Gustavo, Nico, and any team members moving managers tight enough that Monday does not become a scramble.

items.label: Complete the LeadUp Module 2A knowledge check
items.detail: Rippling still shows the LeadUp Module 2A knowledge check as upcoming, with a separate overdue harassment-prevention training still visible. Close the small training task after the transition and KPI items are under control.

sections.title: Today's Schedule
sections.note: Times shown in Jean's Outlook working timezone. The day has a clear execution rhythm: a short deck update in the morning, a transition touchpoint and focus block before lunch, then EOW and executive-status work in the afternoon while Sara, David, and Mar have OOO constraints.

items.label: David OOO and Sara OOO
items.detail: Both show all-day OOO blocks, and Sara also noted in Teams that she is out Friday while keeping the EOW meeting on the calendar if the group wants to connect without her.

items.label: Update Weekly Deck
items.detail: Short update with Mar. Use it to make sure the weekly executive updates reflect the new structure, Sprint #13, and any Chicago-debrief changes that should not wait until Monday.

items.label: CoE Sync Jean is canceled
items.detail: The CoE sync instance is canceled, which leaves room to prepare the First Touchpoint and the midday focus block instead of relying on this slot for Mar/David follow-through.

items.label: First Touchpoint w/Jean
items.detail: Transition conversation with Nicolas, Thiago, and Laura about the new structure and expectations. This is the cleanest live slot for the July 1 alignment work.

items.label: Blocked Space
items.detail: Best focus window of the day. Use it for KPI SMART goals, LeadUp closeout, or final prep for EOW and the executive-status deck.

items.label: EPMO EOW Check In
items.detail: Sara moved this one hour earlier and added Nadia for programmatic updates; Outlook still shows Jean has not responded. Since Sara is OOO, use the slot only if the group can advance updates without her.

items.label: Executive Project Status Slide Update
items.detail: Weekly executive-project slide update. The meeting note says updates now happen Fridays, and Mar declined this instance, so confirm ownership for any CM-CoE, Court, Sprint #13, or Staff Aug changes before the weekend.

items.label: Finish the weekly executive updates before the weekend
items.detail: Sara reminded the Weekly Executive Project Updates group to make Friday updates by end of day or before leaving. Court, CM-CoE, Staff Aug, Sprint #13, and portfolio status changes should be clean enough for team-lead review Monday.

items.label: Confirm the right RACI source before Staff Aug Monday
items.detail: Nadia asked whether the Project RACI is active for new work as Yuri kicks off Staff Augmentation. Janet said the linked version looks outdated and that she and Jorge expect the updated version by the end of the week. Confirm the correct URL or tell Nadia not to use the old file.

items.label: Review Sprint #13 for capacity and priority pressure
items.detail: Mauricio posted the Sprint #13 list for June 26 to July 10. It includes ICE Preparedness Plan items, Court Litify 3.0 escalation work, MHE task-set and file-transfer work, Deadline Management, Case Withdrawal, Forms Automation, and Unresponsive Refunds. Check whether today’s EOW or slide update needs a capacity callout.

items.label: Prepare the COI and Send Letters Queue support path
items.detail: Jhara sent a Tuesday invite and asked for support so COI and Send Letters Queue can move forward using last week’s email feedback. Andrea noted the note also needs Legal feedback and guidance. Keep Jean’s role clear before the Tuesday discussion.

items.label: Decide how to use the IC communication guide
items.detail: Sara forwarded Samantha Holmes’ Inventory Control Communication & Engagement Guide and said she asked whether Samantha might want to join next week’s meeting. Decide whether this belongs in an EPMO discussion and whether Jean needs to review the deck before then.

items.label: Watch Baesman amendment language for SLQ 2.0
items.detail: Baesman replied that amendment language will be supplied for the programs being discussed when quotes are sent. Jean is copied, so no direct reply is obvious yet, but this is the next procurement signal for the SLQ 2.0 path.

items.label: Use EOW only for updates that can move without Sara
items.detail: Sara said she is out Friday and left the EOW meeting on the calendar in case the group wants to connect without her. Keep the meeting practical: collect updates, flag blockers, and avoid decisions that require Sara’s approval.

items.label: Treat overtime interest as exploratory, not approved
items.detail: EPMO Liderazgo showed strong interest in possible overtime capacity, including Jean’s team, but Sara clarified it was only to gauge interest and approvals are not in place yet. Do not plan project capacity as if overtime is available.

items.label: Sara Watson is OOO today
items.detail: Sara’s calendar shows OOO and she reminded the EOW group that she is out Friday, so keep approvals and debrief-dependent decisions light.

items.label: David Molano is OOO today and Monday shows a COL holiday block
items.detail: Outlook shows David OOO today and an OOO - COL Holiday block on Monday. Avoid placing CoE or Staff Aug dependencies on him without confirming availability.

items.label: Mar Garcia is OOO this afternoon
items.detail: Mar has an afternoon OOO block and declined the 3:00 PM Executive Project Status Slide Update, so confirm who owns any CM-CoE slide cleanup.

items.label: Approve Timecards & Prepare for the Week
items.detail: Regular Monday admin block. Good place to clear training, KPI, and transition loose ends before the meeting stack starts.

items.label: Jean Midyear
items.detail: Sara moved midyear reviews to Monday. Have the SMART KPI goals and any team-transition implications ready before this.

items.label: 1:1 Shelley & Jean
items.detail: Department-needs/support conversation immediately after midyear.

items.label: IT - Staff Augmentation MVP Alignment
items.detail: Nadia’s meeting with IT to review the initial MVP proposal and collect feedback. The RACI-source question should be settled before this slot if possible.

items.label: EPMO Portfolio Review
items.detail: Weekly review of new project requests and next-step assignments. Use today’s deck and Sprint #13 cleanup to enter with fewer loose ends.

items.label: PM Feedback
items.detail: Sara said this meeting is for Stephanie Baily to give feedback on Laura; it sounds positive but includes guidance.
